---
title: "Environment (micromamba)"
author: "AY"
---

This workflow uses **micromamba** to create a reproducible environment with all required tools.

## Default environment location

The commands in this repository assume this default path:

```text
~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes
```

On the HPC cluster this expands to:

```text
/fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes
```

The wrapper `./scripts/bcfngs` uses the same path by default. If you keep the environment somewhere else, add `--env-prefix /path/to/env`.

```bash
REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
CLUSTER_ENV_PREFIX = Path("/fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes")
DEFAULT_ENV_PREFIX = CLUSTER_ENV_PREFIX if CLUSTER_ENV_PREFIX.exists() else REPO_ROOT / ".mamba" / "env"
```

## Installing micromamba

micromamba can be installed from the [github repository](https://github.com/mamba-org/micromamba-releases).

```bash
"${SHELL}" <(curl -L https://micro.mamba.pm/install.sh)
```

## Create the workflow environment

From the repo root:

```bash
micromamba create -y -p ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes -f env/environment.yml
```

If your micromamba root prefix is already `~/miniconda3`, the equivalent named-environment command is:

```bash
micromamba create -y -n update_genomes -f env/environment.yml
```

The [environment file](env/environment.yml) states the tools needed to create this environment. We can find there besides the python  packages aslo the various tool we need for the mapping and quantifications as well as file conversions.

## Run commands without activating the environment

For whatever reason we might need, it is also possible to run the tool without activating the environment. 

```bash
micromamba run -p ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes snakemake --version
micromamba run -p ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes STAR --version
```

## Update the environment

If `env/environment.yml` changes:

```bash
micromamba update -y -p ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes -f env/environment.yml
./scripts/bcfngs env check
```

## Health checks

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs env check
```

Example output:

```json
{
  "snakemake": "8.30.0",
  "STAR": "2.7.11b",
  "bwa": "0.7.19-r1273",
  "bowtie2": "2.5.5",
  "samtools": "1.23.1",
  "bamCoverage": "3.5.6" # deeptools
}
```

# How-to (common scenarios)

## First-time setup

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs env create
./scripts/bcfngs env check
```

## Keep tools up to date

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs env update
./scripts/bcfngs env check
```

Optional lockfile:

```bash
mkdir -p env/locks
micromamba list -p ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes --explicit > env/locks/explicit-YYYY-MM-DD.txt
```

## Use HPC modules for vendor tools

With `--profile modules`, the wrapper now resolves tools explicitly instead of relying on whichever binary happens to win on `PATH`.

- `python`, `snakemake`, and `curl` stay in micromamba
- `STAR`, `bwa`, and `bowtie2` are taken from reused or auto-loaded modules
- `samtools` and `bamCoverage` are reused from a preloaded module if one is already active; otherwise they stay in micromamba
- modules-profile commands still print the resolved tool/version report to `stderr`
- plain `./scripts/bcfngs env check` stays JSON-only
- `./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules env check` prints only the tools actually resolved from modules, while keeping the JSON output on `stdout`

Typical usage:

```bash
module load bclconvert/4.3.13 star bowtie2 bwa
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules env check
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules genomes plan
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules genomes build --cores 20
```

Using the `--profile modules` will enbale the analysis using the modules installed via the HPC system. To see the version of modules installed we can test the tool versions:

```bash
$ ./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules env check
Resolved tool versions:
  - STAR: star/2.7.11b 2.7.11b (/fs/pool/pool-cox-modules-software/STAR/2.7.11b/STAR)
  - bwa: bwa/0.7.17 0.7.17-r1198-dirty (/fs/pool/pool-cox-modules-software/bwa/bwa)
  - bowtie2: bowtie2/2.5.4 2.5.4 (/fs/pool/pool-cox-modules-software/bowtie2/2.5.4/bowtie2)
1{
  "snakemake": "8.30.0",
  "STAR": "2.7.11b",
  "bwa": "0.7.17-r1198-dirty",
  "bowtie2": "2.5.4",
  "samtools": "1.23.1",
  "bamCoverage": "3.5.6"
}
```

If we preload a specific module beforehand, this one will be taken automatically into account. 

```bash
$ module load star/2.7.10b
$ ./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules env check
Resolved tool versions:
  - STAR: star/2.7.10b 2.7.10b (/fs/pool/pool-cox-modules-software/STAR/2.7.10b/STAR)
  - bwa: bwa/0.7.17 0.7.17-r1198-dirty (/fs/pool/pool-cox-modules-software/bwa/bwa)
  - bowtie2: bowtie2/2.5.4 2.5.4 (/fs/pool/pool-cox-modules-software/bowtie2/2.5.4/bowtie2)
{
  "snakemake": "8.30.0",
  "STAR": "2.7.10b",
  "bwa": "0.7.17-r1198-dirty",
  "bowtie2": "2.5.4",
  "samtools": "1.23.1",
  "bamCoverage": "3.5.6"
}
```

If you preload an older mapper module, the wrapper will reuse it and require you to **select the matching versioned index** explicitly in mapping:

```bash
module load star/2.7.10b
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules mapping plan \
  --mapper star \
  --star-index-version 2.7.10b
```

### Demultiplexing with `bcl-convert`

This repo does not automate demultiplexing yet. Typical usage on the cluster is:

```bash
module load bclconvert/4.3.13
bcl-convert --help
```

# Troubleshooting

## Missing tools

If `./scripts/bcfngs env check` reports a missing tool:

1. Update the environment:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs env update
./scripts/bcfngs env check
```

2. If you're using a non-default location for your enviroonment, pass it explicitly:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs --env-prefix /path/to/your/env env update
./scripts/bcfngs --env-prefix /path/to/your/env env check
```

3. Verify the package is installed in the expected environment:

```bash
micromamba list -p ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes | grep -Ei 'snakemake|star|bwa|bowtie2|samtools|rseqc'
micromamba run -p ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes snakemake --version
```

4. If needed, recreate the environment:

```bash
rm -rf ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes
./scripts/bcfngs env create
./scripts/bcfngs env check
```
